name: PneUI
short_description: An enabling technology for shape-changing interfaces built from pneumatically actuated soft composite materials that combine input sensing with active shape output.
long_description: PneUI is a fabrication and actuation technique from the MIT Media Lab's Tangible Media Group that builds shape-changing interfaces out of multi-layer soft composite materials whose transformations are driven by pneumatics and a pre-defined internal structure. The composites integrate both input sensing and active shape output, and the design space is demonstrated through applications such as height-changing tangible phicons, a shape-changing mobile, a transformable tablet case, and a shape-shifting lamp. As physical AI and interaction design, it matters as a foundational soft-robotics and tangible-interface technique: it shows how everyday objects and surfaces can computationally change their physical form to convey information and adapt to use, expanding the material vocabulary for embodied interfaces.
ai_note: PneUI is a pneumatic soft-material actuation and sensing technique; shape states are computationally controlled through air pressure and pre-defined structure, with no AI/ML in the system.

images.caption: PneUI shape-changing interface applications built from pneumatically actuated soft composite materials.
images.alt_text: Pneumatically actuated soft objects from the PneUI project, including a shape-shifting lamp and transformable surfaces that inflate and change form.

sources.title: PneUI: Pneumatically Actuated Soft Composite Materials for Shape Changing Interfaces
sources.publisher: MIT Media Lab, Tangible Media Group

sources.title: PneUI: pneumatically actuated soft composite materials for shape changing interfaces

interactions.title: A phicon that rises to mark itself
interactions.description: Set a small tangible token on the table and it inflates to a taller height on command, so its physical stature stands in for a value or state you can read and grab at a glance.

interactions.title: A phone that bends and clips around your wrist
interactions.description: The shape-changing mobile flexes its soft body to curl from a flat slab into a curved cuff, letting the same device sit in your hand or wrap around your arm depending on how you want to carry it.

interactions.title: A tablet case that props itself up
interactions.description: The transformable case inflates a section of its soft composite to lift and angle the tablet into a stand, then deflates flat again to slide back into a bag.

interactions.title: A lamp that breathes into new silhouettes
interactions.description: The shape-shifting lamp slowly morphs its soft pneumatic skin between forms, changing how it casts and shapes light without any moving rigid parts.

interactions.title: Touch it and it knows, then changes
interactions.description: Because the multi-layer composite senses its own bending and touch while pneumatics drive its form, you can press or flex a surface and have it register the input and respond by reshaping — input and shape output living in the same soft material.

interactions.title: Curved-channel folds and textures on demand
interactions.description: By pre-defining internal air channels and stiffness layers, a flat sheet can be programmed to fold along set creases or raise surface bumps when inflated, turning a plain surface into one that can grow tactile texture.

interactions.title: A notification you feel in your pocket's shape
interactions.description: Building on the bendable phone, the device could stiffen or curl slightly to signal an incoming call or alert through a change in its physical form — a tactile, glance-free cue grounded in the same pneumatic shape control it already demonstrates.

interactions.title: An armrest that firms up where you lean
interactions.description: A pneumatic soft surface in a chair or wall could sense pressure through its embedded sensing layer and selectively inflate to support a hand or back exactly where weight lands, using PneUI's combined touch-sensing and shape-output material.
